allegations.text: Air Canada's chatbot told a passenger he could request a bereavement discount retroactively within 90 days after travel. The answer conflicted with Air Canada's bereavement-fare terms, which required approval before travel. The tribunal treated the chatbot answer as part of Air Canada's customer-facing service rather than a separate legal actor.
allegations.mitigation_gap: No effective guardrail prevented the chatbot from presenting a refund rule inconsistent with the canonical policy, and no escalation path corrected the answer before purchase.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Passenger relied on the chatbot answer, bought tickets, and obtained a partial damages award.

allegations.text: Counsel filed a brief containing nonexistent cases, false quotations, and false citations generated through ChatGPT. The lawyer then asked ChatGPT whether the cases were real and relied on the chatbot's answer instead of checking legal databases.
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Federal reporters, legal research databases, and actual case law
allegations.mitigation_gap: No independent cite-checking against legal databases; AI self-verification was substituted for source verification; supervising attorney signed without adequate review.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Underlying lawsuit was dismissed and counsel/firm were sanctioned.

allegations.text: A reply brief cited a case that did not exist. The Second Circuit identified the citation as apparently generated by ChatGPT and referred counsel to the court's grievance panel.
allegations.deployer: Counsel for appellant in Second Circuit appeal
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Second Circuit case law and cited legal authority
allegations.mitigation_gap: No cite-checking caught the nonexistent case before filing; counsel did not ensure AI-assisted legal research matched real authority.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Counsel was referred for possible discipline and the court issued a published warning on AI verification duties.

allegations.text: Attorneys filed briefing containing nonexistent or unsupported case citations attributed to generative AI use. Reporting and case-analysis sources describe a sanctions response after the court identified fabricated authority.
allegations.ai_system_name: ChatGPT / generative AI legal drafting workflow
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Actual case law and court record
allegations.mitigation_gap: AI-generated legal citations were not verified against source materials before filing.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Lawyers were fined and the firm faced sanctions-related scrutiny.

allegations.text: A reply brief contained fabricated quotations and additional misrepresentations. The Fifth Circuit found that AI-generated material had been filed without adequate verification and that counsel's response to the show-cause order compounded the problem.
allegations.ai_system_name: Generative AI, identified in reporting as Casetext/CoCounsel and/or vLex workflow
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Fifth Circuit record, quoted opinions, served Rule 11 materials
allegations.mitigation_gap: No independent verification of quotations or propositions before filing; show-cause response was evasive about AI authorship.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Attorney sanctioned and ordered to pay $2,500 to the clerk.

allegations.text: A post-hearing brief contained fictitious cases, false transcript citations, false Rule 4 file citations, and references to transcript pages that did not exist. Counsel admitted AI use in connection with the brief.
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: ASBCA precedents, transcript volumes, and Rule 4 appeal file
allegations.mitigation_gap: Counsel and staff reviewed the brief but did not verify citations against the actual record or authorities; safeguards were found inadequate.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Board struck the entire reply brief and denied leave to refile.

allegations.text: Class counsel used multiple AI tools to draft and cross-check a class settlement approval brief. The filing contained fabricated quotations from real cases and at least one nonexistent case, and a corrected filing failed to remove the hallucinations.
allegations.ai_system_name: Six unspecified AI tools used concurrently
allegations.deployer: Plaintiff class counsel in Vuori wage-and-hour case
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Actual case law and settlement approval standards
allegations.mitigation_gap: AI tools were used to verify each other instead of checking legal databases; corrected filing did not correct false authority.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Settlement motions were struck; counsel was found inadequate as class counsel; class settlement collapsed into individual resolution.

allegations.text: Attorney filings contained fabricated AI-generated citations in a federal matter. The AI issue was part of a broader disciplinary record that included evasive responses and other noncompliance.
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Actual case law and federal court filing obligations
allegations.mitigation_gap: No citation verification; failure to correct when challenged; broader noncompliance with court and bar processes.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Attorney was suspended in federal court and later disciplined by the Florida Supreme Court.

allegations.text: Application software automatically rejected female applicants age 55 or older and male applicants age 60 or older. The EEOC alleged the screening logic unlawfully denied older applicants an employment opportunity.
allegations.mitigation_gap: No pre-deployment age-discrimination audit or human override prevented hard-coded age thresholds from eliminating qualified applicants.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: More than 200 applicants were allegedly rejected; company settled with EEOC for $365,000 and injunctive relief.

allegations.text: Tenant-screening score allegedly relied heavily on credit history and non-tenancy debt in a way that disadvantaged Black and Hispanic rental applicants using housing vouchers. DOJ and HUD filed a statement of interest supporting Fair Housing Act disparate-impact applicability to algorithmic tenant screening.
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Fair Housing Act and voucher-backed rent-payment facts
allegations.mitigation_gap: No adequate fair-housing disparate-impact testing; model allegedly omitted or undervalued voucher payment certainty.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Voucher holders allegedly denied housing; settlement created monetary and injunctive relief.

allegations.text: MiDAS issued automated unemployment-fraud determinations that were later found overwhelmingly erroneous. Claimants were assessed penalties and collection actions after automated determinations without adequate process.
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Unemployment insurance fraud standards and constitutional due-process requirements
allegations.mitigation_gap: Automated flags lacked meaningful human review and adequate notice/opportunity to contest; error monitoring and escalation failed.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Thousands of claimants were wrongfully accused and faced garnishments, penalties, and collection actions; state settled for $20 million.

allegations.text: Arkansas replaced nurse assessment logic with a computerized RUGs allocation model that reduced home-care hours for many Medicaid beneficiaries. Litigation alleged the system failed to account for individualized conditions and was implemented without proper rulemaking or due process.
allegations.ai_system_name: Resource Utilization Groups (RUGs) computerized care-allocation algorithm
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Medicaid home- and community-based services rules, state APA procedures, individualized care needs
allegations.mitigation_gap: No adequate rulemaking, transparency, individualized validation, or notice process accompanied the algorithmic allocation change.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Disabled beneficiaries lost care hours and challenged the system; state-court rulings and later federal litigation led to relief and reforms.

allegations.text: FTC alleged Rite Aid deployed facial recognition in stores and generated false-positive matches that disproportionately affected certain consumers, including people of color. Employees allegedly acted on false alerts by surveilling, searching, removing, or reporting customers.
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Accurate identity evidence and consumer-protection obligations
allegations.mitigation_gap: Insufficient testing, monitoring, bias controls, employee training, and consumer notice before acting on face-recognition alerts.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Consumers were allegedly wrongly accused, embarrassed, or subjected to law-enforcement contact; FTC order banned Rite Aid from using facial recognition for five years.

allegations.text: A grainy surveillance still was run through facial recognition and returned Robert Williams as a candidate. Police allegedly relied on the output and a flawed photo identification process, leading to a wrongful arrest for a theft he did not commit.
allegations.ai_system_name: Michigan State Police / DataWorks Plus facial-recognition pipeline
allegations.deployer: Detroit Police Department and Michigan State Police
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Actual suspect identity and warrant probable-cause standards
allegations.mitigation_gap: No policy requiring independent corroboration before arrest; warrant materials did not adequately disclose the limits of the face-recognition lead.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Williams was arrested, detained, and later brought a civil-rights suit; settlement included damages and policy reforms.

allegations.text: A blurry fake-ID image was run through facial recognition and returned Nijeer Parks as a possible match. Police allegedly obtained a warrant without adequately verifying alibi evidence or disclosing the uncertainty of the technology.
allegations.ai_system_name: Clearview AI alleged / out-of-state facial-recognition lead
allegations.deployer: Woodbridge Police Department and intelligence-center intermediaries
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Actual suspect identity, alibi evidence, and warrant probable-cause standards
allegations.mitigation_gap: No adequate alibi check, DNA/fingerprint corroboration, or transparent disclosure of FRT provenance before arrest.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Parks spent about 10 days in jail before charges were dismissed; civil-rights litigation followed.

allegations.text: Detroit police used a facial-recognition lead to place Porcha Woodruff in a lineup for a carjacking investigation. The suit alleges obvious exculpatory circumstances, including pregnancy, were not adequately reconciled before arrest.
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Actual suspect identity and warrant probable-cause standards
allegations.mitigation_gap: FRT output seeded the lineup without sufficient independent corroboration or review of distinguishing characteristics.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Woodruff was arrested while pregnant, detained, and later sued after charges were dropped.

allegations.text: A private retail loss-prevention workflow allegedly matched robbery footage to old mugshots of Harvey Murphy and communicated certainty to police. Murphy alleged the match and lineup process caused his arrest despite not being the robber.
allegations.deployer: EssilorLuxottica / Macy's loss-prevention personnel and Houston police
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: Actual suspect identity and reliable biometric-comparison standards
allegations.mitigation_gap: Private FRT comparison lacked qualified-examiner controls; old photographs and employee pre-briefing allegedly contaminated the identification process; police allegedly did not independently investigate.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Murphy was detained and alleges severe jailhouse harm; civil case seeks damages.

allegations.text: Plaintiff alleges Workday's AI screening tools caused discriminatory job-application rejections based on age, race, and disability-related factors. The case is included as a vendor-liability candidate because the public record identifies AI screening and a court allowed key claims to proceed, but discrete error rows may need to be split by plaintiff or application event.
allegations.ai_system_name: Workday HiredScore / Candidate Skills Match tools
allegations.deployer: Workday, Inc. as HR software vendor
allegations.canonical_source_conflicted: ADEA, Title VII, ADA, FEHA, and employer-specific applicant records
allegations.mitigation_gap: Alleged lack of disparate-impact audit, biased training/preferences, and absence of meaningful human review before rejection.
allegations.reliance_or_harm: Plaintiff alleges repeated job rejections through Workday systems; ADEA collective certification was reported.
